title: One-Pot Dal Chawal

subs.name: 50% rice + 50% broken wheat (dalia) for a high-fibre version
subs.notes: soak the dalia for 10 minutes first

subs.name: masoor dal (red lentil)
subs.notes: same cooking time — adds an earthy reddish colour

subs.name: omit for a simpler khichdi
subs.notes: add a pinch more asafoetida if omitting

steps.text: Rinse rice and moong dal together until water is mostly clear. Set aside to drain.
Heat oil in the pressure cooker on medium. Add cumin seeds and sizzle 10 seconds. Add onion, garlic, and ginger and fry until the onion is translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add tomato, turmeric, and chilli powder. Stir and cook until tomato breaks down, about 2 minutes. Season with salt.

Add the drained rice and moong dal, pour in 3 cups water, stir once, and close the pressure cooker lid. Cook on high until 3 whistles, then turn off the heat and let pressure release naturally — at least 10 minutes.

Open the cooker and gently stir — the texture should be porridge-like (khichdi style), with dal and rice merged. Taste for salt, scatter coriander, and serve with pickle and papad for a complete meal.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: moong-dal → masoor dal (red lentil) (same cooking time — adds an earthy reddish colour); rice → 50% rice + 50% broken wheat (dalia) for a high-fibre version (soak the dalia for 10 minutes first); onion → omit for a simpler khichdi (add a pinch more asafoetida if omitting)
